_note: Batch 11, sub-batch 1 of ~3. Two categories: youth_summer_9_12 (2 candidates: JCC Maccabi Israel Sports Tour, Technion Ignite -- both grades 9-12, Israel-based, official site confirmed) and hesder_yeshivot_batch1 (12 of ~43 Israeli-audience hesder/gevoha yeshivot, sourced from the Igud Yeshivot ha-Hesder official member directory, hesder.org.il, cross-checked against the DB's existing ~68 mostly-overseas-student yeshivot). One candidate (Tzfat, tsfat.org.il) was DROPPED mid-research: the domain turned out to belong to a cultural/tourism organization ('Ruach Tzfat', promoting the city's culture with tours and festivals), not the hesder yeshiva itself -- a wrong domain match caught before writing anything, not an inference. Two branch relationships were discovered and preserved as distinct rows (matching the Otzem/Ke'Ayal precedent from the mechinot batch): Chiburim Afula is an explicit branch of Chiburim Beit Shean (same site, dedicated subpage), and Hesder Yeshiva Akko is an explicit branch of Maalot Yaakov (its own dedicated domain). Per explicit user policy: every field sourced from each program's own official site only; no price/tuition; no specific session dates. durationType is CUSTOM for the hesder yeshivot (not GAP_YEAR) since hesder is structurally a multi-year combined Torah-study/IDF-service track, not a single year -- none of these pages stated an exact year count so durationText is left blank rather than assuming the generic '5-year hesder' figure. Ayelet HaShachar (Eilat) returned HTTP 403 on direct fetch (bot-blocked); description is limited to what was independently confirmed (real institution, contact email, Eilat location) rather than guessed.

youth_summer_9_12.name: JCC Maccabi Israel Sports Tour
youth_summer_9_12.description: A new Israel trip launching in 2026 connecting participants to Israel through the lens of sports, with unique encounters and site visits. Distinct from the JCC Maccabi Games in Israel (held every four years), which combines a week of athletic competition with two weeks touring Israel.

youth_summer_9_12.name: Technion Ignite: International High School Summer Program
youth_summer_9_12.description: A summer program for international high school students to develop entrepreneurial skills and gain knowledge in science and technology fields at the Technion campus. Eligibility requires being at least 17 years of age with a strong STEM background and demonstrated interest in innovation and entrepreneurship.

hesder_yeshivot_batch1.name: Yeshivat Or Etzion
hesder_yeshivot_batch1.description: A high-level yeshiva founded in the spirit of Rabbi Avraham Isaac Kook, established in 1977 by Rabbi Haim Druckman, emphasizing Torah study, fear of Heaven, observance of commandments, love of the Jewish people, and love of the State of Israel. Also houses a preparatory track for the Ethiopian Jewish community (Or Meofir), a conversion center, and a teacher training college.

hesder_yeshivot_batch1.name: Yeshivat HaGolan
hesder_yeshivot_batch1.description: A higher yeshiva with a hesder track in the Golan Heights, offering study hall lessons in Gemara, Halacha, faith and philosophy, mussar, and Biblical studies, with an active alumni network and Torah library.

hesder_yeshivot_batch1.name: Yeshivat Har Bracha
hesder_yeshivot_batch1.description: A Torah institution centered on 'Torah as a way of life,' led by Rabbi Eliezer Melamed, offering a Kollel for rabbinical studies, a Shiluvim track combining Torah study with secular university degrees, a women's Torat Chaim Midrasha, and a program for army veterans.

hesder_yeshivot_batch1.name: Yeshivat Tfachot
hesder_yeshivot_batch1.description: A hesder yeshiva established in 1980 to strengthen Jewish presence in the Galilee, combining Torah study with military service. About 200 students currently study there; the institution also runs mission-oriented communities (garinim) in Tfachot and Tiberias, plus an extension yeshiva, Ramat Arbel.

hesder_yeshivot_batch1.name: Yeshivat Hesder Maalot Yaakov
hesder_yeshivot_batch1.description: A Bnei Akiva-affiliated hesder yeshiva in Maalot centered on the doctrine of 'Torah of the Land of Israel,' combining religious study with Zionist values. Runs a 'Bnei Menashe' integration project for that diaspora community and a 'Panim el Panim' interpersonal engagement initiative.

hesder_yeshivot_batch1.name: Yeshivat Netzer Matai (Ariel)
hesder_yeshivot_batch1.description: A Torah study center in Ariel following the teachings of Rabbi Kook and his son, offering deep learning for gap-year students (week-long intensive tracks) and flexible courses for university students. Originally established in Netzarim, Gush Katif, before relocating to Ariel.

hesder_yeshivot_batch1.name: Yeshivat Shirat Moshe (Meirim BeYafo)
hesder_yeshivot_batch1.description: A yeshiva founded by Rabbi Eliyahu Mali as the centerpiece of the Meirim BeYafo organization, which runs about nine initiatives in Jaffa including a pre-military academy (Mechinat Chivat Tzion), a women's center, and student housing, aiming to strengthen Jewish communal life in the city. Around 600 students and volunteers participate across the organization's institutions.

hesder_yeshivot_batch1.name: Yeshivat HaSeder Nof HaGalil
hesder_yeshivot_batch1.description: A hesder yeshiva in Nof HaGalil emphasizing deep Torah study with independent thinking, national unity through exploring Jewish peoplehood, and character development including leadership and personal responsibility.

hesder_yeshivot_batch1.name: Hesder Yeshiva Akko
hesder_yeshivot_batch1.description: A branch of Hesder Yeshiva Maalot Yaakov established in 2002 by Rabbi Yossi Stern, combining Torah study with active community involvement and volunteer work in Akko. Runs a 'Netiv' course integrating IDF soldiers and programs for local university students.

hesder_yeshivot_batch1.name: Yeshivat Chiburim Beit Shean
hesder_yeshivot_batch1.description: A hesder yeshiva operated by the Chiburim Beit Shean VeHaEmek organization, which also runs family support and counseling services, agricultural education, and adult education (midrasha) programs across the Beit Shean valley region, in partnership with several government ministries and municipalities.

hesder_yeshivot_batch1.name: Yeshivat Chiburim Afula
hesder_yeshivot_batch1.description: A branch of the Chiburim Beit Shean VeHaEmek hesder yeshiva, operating in Afula alongside the main Beit Shean institution under the same organizational umbrella.

hesder_yeshivot_batch2.name: Yeshivat Kiryat Shmona
hesder_yeshivot_batch2.description: A settlement hesder yeshiva in northern Israel, established in 1977 by Rabbi Tzfanya Drori (a student of Rabbi Tzvi Yehuda HaCohen Kook) to strengthen Torah values in the northern border region. About 230 students engage in diverse learning methods; the yeshiva positions itself as a Torah center for the Upper Galilee.

hesder_yeshivot_batch2.name: Yeshivat Tekoa
hesder_yeshivot_batch2.description: A yeshiva operating in the spirit of Rabbi Adin Steinsaltz z"l, emphasizing in-depth and broad Talmudic study to develop Torah scholars grounded in tradition. Also runs a kollel for advanced study.

hesder_yeshivot_batch2.name: Yeshivat HaHesder Rishon LeZion
hesder_yeshivot_batch2.description: A family-oriented hesder yeshiva focused on developing students' spiritual identity as Torah scholars, combining in-depth Talmud study with Jewish thought, life skills, mentorship, and community engagement.

hesder_yeshivot_batch2.name: Yeshivat Ramat Gan
hesder_yeshivot_batch2.description: A religious-Zionist Chassidic hesder yeshiva, described as a pioneer among Zionist yeshivot in the Gush Dan area, established in 1994 (5754). Led by Rabbi Yehoshua Shapira, joined by Rabbi Ben Tzion Elgazi since 2018; Rabbi Yaakov Ariel (former city rabbi) serves as president.

hesder_yeshivot_batch2.name: Beit Midrash Reuta (Carmel)
hesder_yeshivot_batch2.description: A yeshiva founded in 2000 in Moshav Carmel offering both a higher-yeshiva track and a hesder track, plus a kollel for married scholars, led by founder Rabbi Natan Ophner (a graduate of Yeshivat Merkaz HaRav). Curriculum covers Talmud (Bava Batra, Gittin), Jewish philosophy (Rav Kook, Maimonides, the Kuzari), and weekly Torah portions, offered via in-person lessons, live-streamed classes, and a phone/audio lecture line.

hesder_yeshivot_batch2.name: Yeshivat HaBikah (Sdemot Neriah)
hesder_yeshivot_batch2.location: Sdemot Mechola, northern Jordan Valley, Israel
hesder_yeshivot_batch2.description: A hesder yeshiva named for Rabbi Moshe Tzvi Neriah, established in 1994 by Yeshivat Bnei Akiva Kfar Haroeh (Rabbi Neriah's last founding before his passing a year later), relocated to Sdemot Mechola to strengthen Jewish settlement in the Jordan Valley. About 40 students study there under current heads Rabbi Nehemiah Ranan and Rabbi Avi'ad Shinuval.
hesder_yeshivot_batch2._sourcingNote: ybikah.com was unreachable at research time (DNS failure on repeated attempts); description sourced from WebSearch summaries that quoted the official site's own content, not directly re-verified by fetch.

hesder_yeshivot_batch2.name: Yeshivat Sdot Negev (Kfar Maimon)
hesder_yeshivot_batch2.description: A hesder yeshiva open to high school graduates from throughout Israel, combining Torah study with meaningful military service, led by the rabbi of Kibbutz Sa'ad, Rabbi Ari Set, and Rabbi Michael Adre'i (rabbi of Kfar Maimon-Toushiya).
hesder_yeshivot_batch2._sourcingNote: ysn.org.il returned HTTP 503 (explicit Retry-After) at research time; description sourced from WebSearch summaries quoting the official site, not directly re-verified by fetch.

hesder_yeshivot_batch2.name: Yeshivat Karnei Shomron
hesder_yeshivot_batch2.description: One of the veteran hesder yeshivot, established in 1980 (5740), located in western Samaria near Kedumim, Emanuel, and Alfei Menashe. Led by Rabbi Shmuel Haber and Rabbi Avraham Kurzweil.
hesder_yeshivot_batch2._sourcingNote: karnash.co.il timed out at research time; description sourced from WebSearch summaries quoting the official site, not directly re-verified by fetch.

hesder_yeshivot_batch2.name: Yeshivat Or Akiva
hesder_yeshivot_batch2.description: A hesder yeshiva complex in Or Akiva encompassing a preparatory program, the yeshiva itself, a girls' school, and an alumni program, with about 120 students. Led by Rabbi Yossi Alfasi. Approach emphasizes meeting each student where he is, with deep learning rooted in love and faith, journeys, and personal guidance.

hesder_yeshivot_batch2.name: Yeshivat Hesder Orot Yaakov (Rehovot)
hesder_yeshivot_batch2.description: A hesder yeshiva in Rehovot founded roughly 50 years ago, combining Torah study in a warm, family-like atmosphere with military service preparation and an academic degree track. About 200 students study there under Rabbi Ami Danino.
hesder_yeshivot_batch2._sourcingNote: orotyaakov.org.il was unreachable at research time (DNS failure); description sourced from WebSearch summaries quoting the official site, not directly re-verified by fetch.

hesder_yeshivot_batch2.name: Yeshivat Orot Shaul (Tel Aviv)
hesder_yeshivot_batch2.description: A hesder yeshiva founded in September 1998 by Rabbi Yuval Sherlo, Rabbi David Stav, and Rabbi Shai Piron, led by Rabbi Yuval Sherlo, Rabbi Tamir Granot, and Rabbi Itamar Eldar. Relocated to Tel Aviv's Shapira neighborhood in 2020, at which point it left the AMIT network and joined the Ruach HaGolan institutions.

hesder_yeshivot_batch2.name: Yeshiva Gevoha Itamar
hesder_yeshivot_batch2.description: A higher yeshiva established in 1998 on a hill in the Itamar settlement overlooking Shechem, founded by Rabbi Avihai Rontzky and now led by Rabbi Yehoshua van Dijk. Publishes a quarterly Torah journal, 'Ha'Tomer,' and maintains student groups worldwide.

hesder_yeshivot_batch2.name: Yeshivat Birkat Yosef (Alon Moreh)
hesder_yeshivot_batch2.description: A hesder and higher-yeshiva in Alon Moreh founded in 1995 (5755), with over 180 students and avrechim across its hesder and gevoha tracks. Led by Rabbi Eliyakim Levanon and Rabbi Shachar Eimber.

hesder_yeshivot_batch2.name: Yeshivat Ashkelon (Orot HaTorah VeHaChesed)
hesder_yeshivot_batch2.description: A hesder-shiluvim yeshiva in Ashkelon educating students toward love of Torah, the Jewish people, and the State of Israel, combining significant military service with a mission-oriented outlook. Offers a post-army teaching-degree track in partnership with Orot Israel College.
hesder_yeshivot_batch2._sourcingNote: yashkelon.co.il was unreachable at research time (DNS failure); description sourced from WebSearch summaries quoting the official site, not directly re-verified by fetch.

hesder_yeshivot_batch2.name: Yeshivat Shilo
hesder_yeshivot_batch2.description: A Torah study institution in Shilo emphasizing deep Talmudic study from foundational understanding through advanced rabbinic sources, complemented by courses on faith, ethics, and Hasidic teachings. About 100 students and post-graduate scholars, plus military groups, led by Rabbi Michael Broom (president) and Rabbi Aviv Gamliel (head); a separate kollel of ~25 advanced scholars is headed by Rabbi Chen Yitzhak.

hesder_yeshivot_batch3.name: Beit Orot (Jerusalem)
hesder_yeshivot_batch3.description: A Beit Midrash on Mount Scopus for army graduates seeking to dedicate a period to intensive Torah study before continuing their life course, led by Rabbi Chen David Hallamish. Also runs a Jerusalem Brigade-Mount Scopus visitor center with tours and lectures, and community programming around settlement in East Jerusalem.

hesder_yeshivot_batch3.name: Yeshivat HaMeiri (Kiryat Moshe)
hesder_yeshivot_batch3.description: A hesder yeshiva in Jerusalem's Kiryat Moshe neighborhood, established in 1995 at the direction of former Chief Rabbi Mordechai Eliyahu, founded by Rabbi Shmuel Zafrani and Dr. Yaakov Hadani, offering a unique track that combines yeshiva study with academic studies leading to accreditation. About 80 students study there.
hesder_yeshivot_batch3._sourcingNote: yhm10.org returned HTTP 403 on this fetch attempt; description sourced from an earlier WebSearch summary quoting the official site directly.

hesder_yeshivot_batch3.name: Yeshivat Or VeYeshua (Haifa)
hesder_yeshivot_batch3.description: A hesder and higher yeshiva founded in 2001 by Rabbi Dr. Eliyahu Zayni as the first Zionist yeshiva in Haifa, combining meaningful Torah study with military service, emphasizing personal growth, responsibility, and love for the Jewish people and Land of Israel.

hesder_yeshivot_batch3.name: Yeshivat Midbara Ke'Eden (Mitzpe Ramon)
hesder_yeshivot_batch3.description: One of Israel's larger hesder yeshivot, established in 2000 and deliberately located in Mitzpe Ramon to advance settlement and spiritual revival in the Negev. About 300 students, including roughly 80 married students, led by Rabbi Tzvi Kostiner; also runs a smaller sister high-school-age yeshiva, Midbara Ke'Eden LeTze'irim. Graduates have founded Torah communities in Arad, Gitit, Givat Shmuel, and elsewhere, serving as rabbis, educators, and military officers.

hesder_yeshivot_batch3.name: Yeshivat Givat Olga (Hadera)
hesder_yeshivot_batch3.description: A high school and hesder yeshiva in the Givat Olga neighborhood of Hadera, founded in 2011 through the initiative of the Torah nucleus 'Atid Im Tikva,' and formally recognized as a hesder yeshiva by the Defense Ministry in 2013. Led by Rabbi Efraim Giami. Students volunteer in personal mentoring, hospital visits, and community events.
hesder_yeshivot_batch3._sourcingNote: yeshiva-olga.com was unreachable at research time (DNS failure); description sourced from an earlier WebSearch summary quoting the official site directly.

hesder_yeshivot_batch3.name: Yeshivat HaHesder Dimona
hesder_yeshivot_batch3.description: A higher/hesder yeshiva in Dimona established roughly 12 years ago, led by Rabbi David Torjeman, taking responsibility for Torah leadership in the city. Encourages enlistment in combat roles within the hesder track as well as special forces and reconnaissance units; the hesder track runs 17 months of service.

hesder_yeshivot_batch3.name: Yeshivat Derech Chaim (Kiryat Gat)
hesder_yeshivot_batch3.description: A hesder yeshiva in Kiryat Gat established in 2006 (5766), known for its in-depth analytical approach and high level of Talmudic and halachic study. About 150 students, including about 20 avrechim (married scholars) and 130 unmarried students, led by Rabbi Amir Maman.

hesder_yeshivot_batch3.name: Yeshivat Cholon
hesder_yeshivot_batch3.description: A hesder yeshiva in Holon (population 200,000+) founded to establish a significant Torah center in the city, combining Talmudic study, Jewish law, biblical texts, and philosophy with military-service preparation and 'Lore of the Land of Israel.' Most students follow a 5-year track (2 years yeshiva, 1.5 years service, 1.5 years yeshiva); an alternative 3-year full-service track is also offered.
hesder_yeshivot_batch3.durationText: 5 years (2yr yeshiva + 1.5yr service + 1.5yr yeshiva), or a 3-year full-service track

hesder_yeshivot_batch3.name: Yeshivat Ahavat Israel (Netivot)
hesder_yeshivot_batch3.description: A hesder yeshiva in Netivot established in 2000, with about 50 students (roughly 20 in active military service), founded by Rabbi Israel Friedman Ben-Shalom together with his son Shmuli Ben-Shalom, with Rabbi David Asulin serving as head of yeshiva.
hesder_yeshivot_batch3._sourcingNote: yhn.co.il returned HTTP 404 on all paths tried at research time; description sourced from an earlier WebSearch summary quoting the official site directly.

hesder_yeshivot_batch3.name: Yeshivat Orot Moshe (Rosh HaAyin)
hesder_yeshivot_batch3.description: A hesder-shiluvim yeshiva in Rosh HaAyin led by Rabbi Gilad Chala, combining faith-based Torah study with meaningful military service across a four-year structure: a year of yeshiva study preparing for the army, two years of military service, and a final year of yeshiva study preparing for life after service.
hesder_yeshivot_batch3._sourcingNote: orotmoshe.com was unreachable at research time (DNS failure); description sourced from an earlier WebSearch summary quoting the official site directly.

hesder_yeshivot_batch3.name: Yeshivat HaHesder Ramla
hesder_yeshivot_batch3.description: A hesder yeshiva in Ramla established in 2014 (Elul 5774), located in a complex on the edge of the market in the Emishav neighborhood, combining higher Torah study with significant military service. Led by Rabbi Ari Hendler.

hesder_yeshivot_batch3.name: Yeshivat HaHesder Ramat HaSharon
hesder_yeshivot_batch3.description: A hesder yeshiva in Ramat HaSharon led by Rabbi Moshe Chai B'Harabi, with three main study tracks: in-depth Talmud study in the morning, breadth of study in the afternoons and evenings, and Halacha study for married students. Views military service through the hesder track as an expression of the value of return to Zion and public responsibility.

hesder_yeshivot_batch3.name: Yeshivat Nachalat Yosef (Shavei Shomron)
hesder_yeshivot_batch3.description: A religious-nationalist yeshiva in Shavei Shomron with both a higher-yeshiva and hesder track, led by Rabbi Yehoshua Shmidt with Rabbi Dov Lior as president. Renamed Yeshivat Nachalat Yosef in 2017 in memory of Rabbi Yosef Schwartz. Known for studying Talmud using the 'Method of Signs' (Shitat HaSimanim), a mnemonic system developed by Rabbi Shmidt.

hesder_yeshivot_batch3.name: Yeshivat Sderot
hesder_yeshivot_batch3.description: A hesder yeshiva in Sderot combining Talmudic study with Jewish thought (Rav Kook, the Maharal) and national-religious/Zionist ideology, operating dormitory facilities and community outreach projects. Runs a 'Lev LaDaat' branch/track (about 150 students total across the institution) alongside its main programming.
hesder_yeshivot_batch3._dedupResolution: Confirmed distinct from the existing DB entry yeshivat-hesder-sderot (friendsofsderot.org, founded 1995 by Rabbi Dovid Fendel, 800+ students) -- different domain, different founder/leadership named, different scale. Sderot appears to have more than one hesder yeshiva, same pattern already seen at Kiryat Gat (two distinct yeshivot).

hesder_yeshivot_batch3.name: Yeshivat HaSeder Gavoha Kiryat Gat
hesder_yeshivot_batch3.description: A hesder yeshiva in Kiryat Gat established to disseminate Torah with love among the Jewish people, distinct from the city's other hesder yeshiva (Derech Chaim). Emphasizes divine service with love and joy, love for the Jewish people, passionate Torah study, community responsibility, and character development.
